title: urtica gracilis subsp. incaica
intro: Urtica gracilis subsp. incaica, also known as Inca nettle, is a subspecies of the Urtica gracilis plant, a perennial nettle native to the Andean region. It is rich in medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Inca nettle include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Inca nettle is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
